description:
You are a financial analyst. Open novapeak_historicals.xlsx from ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/. It contains three years (Year 1-3) of historical income statement and balance sheet data for NovaPeak Analytics, a B2B SaaS company.

Build a 5-year Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation model with the following sheets:

Assumptions: Derive operating margins (COGS, Sales & Marketing, R&D, G&A as percent of revenue), D&A rate, CapEx rate, and working capital ratios from Year 3 historical data. Note: Year 2 includes a one-time $500,000 restructuring charge that should be excluded from your normalized analysis. Management projects 20% annual revenue growth for Years 4 through 8.

Projections: Project the full income statement for Years 4-8 using the derived margins applied to projected revenue. Project balance sheet working capital items using historical ratios. Tax rate is 25%.

FCF: Compute annual Unlevered Free Cash Flow for each projected year: FCF = EBIT x (1 - Tax Rate) + D&A - CapEx - Change in Net Working Capital.

DCF: Discount each year's FCF at a WACC of 10.5%. Compute terminal value using the Gordon Growth Model with a 2.5% perpetual growth rate applied to Year 8 FCF. Sum the present values of all FCFs and the terminal value to arrive at the Enterprise Value. Display the Enterprise Value prominently.

Save as novapeak_dcf_model.xlsx in ~/Documents/Spreadsheets/.

metadata.data_source: Synthetic SaaS financials calibrated to industry benchmarks
